findings.title: completion.ts merged into window.ts, not advance.ts as spec requires
findings.detail: The proposal explicitly states: 'task-planner/completion.ts + task-planner/window.ts → inline into task-planner/advance.ts (their only production caller)'. The diff instead merges completion.ts into window.ts. Either the spec or the implementation needs to be corrected to match.
findings.notes: Stale finding. The proposal was corrected during design phase (design.md D3 erratum): advance.ts does NOT import from completion.ts — window.ts is the actual sole production importer. The current proposal.md explicitly states 'absorbed into src/lib/task-planner/window.ts (its only production importer), not advance.ts'. Implementation matches the corrected proposal.

findings.title: phase-router/errors.ts → router.ts merge missing from diff
findings.detail: The proposal specifies 'phase-router/errors.ts (50 lines, error classes only) → inline into phase-router/router.ts; phase-router/index.ts keeps re-exporting the same classes.' This consolidation is not present in the diff.
findings.notes: Stale finding. The proposal was corrected during design phase (design.md D3): errors.ts has TWO internal consumers (router.ts and derive-action.ts), violating the single-consumer precondition. The current proposal.md explicitly states 'src/lib/phase-router/errors.ts is NOT merged'. Intentionally absent from the diff.

findings.title: Spec retirement not visible in diff
findings.detail: The proposal requires removing the 'setup rerun performs deterministic diff-and-resolve on existing profile' requirement from project-bootstrap-installation/spec.md. This change is not in the diff. Verify it is staged or pending.
findings.notes: The spec delta exists at openspec/changes/librefactoring/specs/project-bootstrap-installation/spec.md with a REMOVED Requirements section. Per the OpenSpec workflow, the baseline spec is rewritten at archive time — the delta is the correct mechanism. The proposal explicitly states: 'the baseline spec file is rewritten by OpenSpec at archive time'. The delta file is present in the changeset (untracked under openspec/changes/librefactoring/).

findings.title: Test deletion for profile-diff not visible in diff
findings.detail: The proposal says 'src/tests/interaction-records.test.ts is currently the only confirmed match (sole subject = profile-diff.ts) and is deleted.' This deletion is not in the provided diff. Verify it is included in the full changeset.
findings.notes: Stale finding. The proposal was corrected during design phase (design.md D1 erratum): interaction-records.test.ts imports in-memory-interaction-record-store.ts and types/interaction-records.ts, NOT profile-diff.ts. The current proposal.md explicitly states: 'zero tests match' and 'interaction-records.test.ts imports in-memory-interaction-record-store.ts (not touched) rather than profile-diff.ts, so it is NOT deleted'. No test file deletion is required.

findings.title: send-queue.ts → session-manager.ts merge not explicitly confirmed in spec
findings.detail: The proposal says agent-session merges proceed 'only if design confirms each candidate has a single internal consumer'. The merge of SendQueue into session-manager.ts appears reasonable but the spec's confirmed list only names task-planner and phase-router consolidations. Ensure design phase confirmed this merge.
findings.notes: The current proposal.md explicitly lists this merge: 'src/lib/agent-session/send-queue.ts (43 lines) → absorbed into src/lib/agent-session/session-manager.ts (its only production importer)'. Design phase confirmed send-queue.ts has a single consumer (session-manager.ts). The merge is in the confirmed list.

findings.title: Inlined SendQueue uses mutation pattern
findings.detail: SendQueue mutates this.poisonError and this.tail in place. This is acceptable for a queue's internal state machine, but note it conflicts with the project's immutability-first coding style rule. The original send-queue.ts had the same pattern, so this is not a regression — just flagging for awareness.
findings.notes: Acknowledged. Not a regression — the original send-queue.ts had identical mutation patterns. Queue internal state machines inherently require mutable state for tail-chaining and poison tracking. The coding style rule notes language-specific overrides are acceptable when idiomatically required.

findings.title: Deleted source files not included in diff
findings.detail: Git status shows send-queue.ts, completion.ts, ecosystem-detector.ts, and profile-diff.ts as unstaged deletions (` D`), meaning they are deleted in the working tree but not staged. The diff under review does not capture these deletions. The consolidation targets (session-manager.ts, window.ts) already inline the code, but until the old files are staged for deletion and verified absent, the refactoring is incomplete. Stage the deletions (`git add` the removed files) and confirm the build passes with the files gone.

findings.title: barrel-equivalence.test.ts is untracked and not in diff
findings.detail: The proposal explicitly requires a new `src/tests/barrel-equivalence.test.ts` to pin the three barrels' runtime export surfaces. Git status shows it as `??` (untracked) but its contents are not part of the reviewed diff. This test is a key safety net for the refactoring — it must be reviewed for correctness and included in the final changeset.
